Web search confirmed Re=1×10^6 is standard for wind turbine airfoil analysis at moderate to high Reynolds numbers and is compatible with XFoil analysis (PMC research articles and QBlade forums document Re=10^6 as typical operating condition)
This range is typical for wind turbine airfoil analysis including stall onset and normal operating region. No authoritative specification contradicts this value.
This range is typical for wind turbine airfoil analysis including post-stall behavior. No authoritative specification contradicts this value.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires analyzing three real NACA airfoils (NACA 0015: symmetric 15% thickness; NACA 2412: 2% camber 12% thickness; NACA 4412: 4% camber 12% thickness) using XFoil at Reynolds number 1,000,000 (standard for wind turbine analysis) across an angle of attack range of -5° to 20°. The airfoil coordinate files are standard NACA database files verified by direct analysis showing correct thickness and camber properties. All expected output filenames and parameters are consistent with aerodynamic engineering standards for wind turbine blade design evaluation.
